judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with solid, well-grounded evidence; Report 1 is slightly more thorough on the 40%/production-data flags while Report 2 adds a nuanced but unnecessary caveat about attribution policy, making them roughly comparable in quality.

report_1.evidence: "Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default." (line 30) — FALSE, contradicted by code.** `app/security/config.py:3-4` reads: `# Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic.` and `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`... "Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF." (line 24) — FALSE, feature not built.** `app/routers/visits.py` has only a `list_visits` endpoint that returns `[]` (line 8-9) and an explicit `# TODO: pdf export` (line 11). There is no PDF export. **Must be removed.**

report_2.evidence: "Stronger account security — Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default." — False; correct or remove.** `app/security/config.py` states the opposite: `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic."... "Export your visit history — Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF." — False (not built); remove.** `app/routers/visits.py` has only `list_visits` returning `[]` and a literal `# TODO: pdf export`.

report_1.evidence: The "after" image does not show the scheduling calendar — it shows the **billing/invoices page**... `captures.json` line 3: `"after_scheduling.png": {"route": "/billing/invoices", "state": "seed-demo"}` — the file was captured from `/billing/invoices`, not the calendar.

report_2.evidence: The "after" image is a different screen than the "before." I opened both: `before_scheduling.png` reads "SCHEDULING CALENDAR," but `after_scheduling.png` reads "BILLING / INVOICES." This is confirmed independently by `captures.json`, which maps `after_scheduling.png` to route `/billing/invoices`

report_1.evidence: **`after_export.png` — referenced but missing (broken image).** The doc embeds it at line 26, but no such file exists in `img/`, and `captures.json` line 6 confirms: `"state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"`... **`unused_capture_07.png` — present but orphaned.** It exists in `img/` but is not referenced by the document.

report_2.evidence: **Referenced but missing: `img/after_export.png`.**... no such file exists in `docs/release/img/`... **Exists but unreferenced (orphan): `img/unused_capture_07.png`.** This file is on disk (it shows "PATIENT LIST") but is not referenced anywhere in the document

report_1.evidence: **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance." (line 33)** is an internal drafting note. It has no place in a polished customer-facing announcement... Remove it.

report_2.evidence: **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** This is an internal process note that does not belong in a customer-facing email... at minimum it should not ship in the customer email.
